response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced software developer with a broad web and full-stack background, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position has two near-absolute requirements he does not meet: native-level Japanese proficiency (he lists English, Romanian, and German only) and strong C programming for embedded UNIX/Linux development (his primary stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang with only incidental C/C++ use at National Instruments). He has no demonstrated experience in LAN-switch firmware, embedded networking security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), Linux kernel development, or any of the listed security domains. His embedded exposure is limited to robotic arm software and does not transfer to network security firmware work.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development across diverse domains demonstrates solid engineering fundamentals
Hands-on Linux usage and some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments provides a minimal baseline
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems at Sociomantic shows performance-aware engineering mindset
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement for this role
No experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, L2/L3 protocols, switch SDKs)
Linux kernel development and source-code modification capability is absent; his Linux use is at the application layer only
